title: eriotheca parvifolia
intro: Eriotheca parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved catclaw, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, the plant's roots and bark are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriotheca parvifolia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as wounds and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the roots and bark of the plant are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
